授業目的: Startup Week Online Lecture, April 3, 2026 - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3NxUnOoGYOsThis course is designed to familiarize students with:1. the >HISTORY< of international conflict (with a focus on Europe from ancient times to the Cold War era);2. the particulars of specific conflicts and the broader structural features that often transcend specific conflicts; and 3. the way that history has shaped theoretical models that underpin international relations as a field of study.Note 1: This course does NOT discuss the current conflicts in Ukraine or in the Middle East.Note 2: This is a very challenging course with a **heavy reading and writing workload.**
到達目標: Students will learn about actual cases of international conflict and fundamental concepts in international relations theory.
関連科目: Global Politics, International Relations, Conflict and Peace Studies, International Security, Modern History, International Organizations in World Politics.
授業の概要・背景:
This course focuses on selected cases of interstate and intrastate conflict from various time periods in European history. 

Note 1: This course does NOT discuss the current conflicts in Ukraine or in the Middle East.
Note 2: This is a very challenging course with a **heavy reading and writing workload.**
授業方法: >>IMPORTANT<< This course is primarily in face-to-face format, but some lectures may be in on-demand (online) format -- which lectures are in person and which are online is subject to change, so please make sure to regularly check this course's LUNA page for announcements about any changes to the course schedule.

教科書/Required texts0:
The following two articles are available online (the URL will be provided on LUNA):
Francois de Soete, “The Limits of Interpretation and Thucydides: An Analysis of Realist Themes in the History of the Peloponnesian War,” The Journal of International Studies 13 (2024): 33-47. Available online at: https://kwansei.repo.nii.ac.jp/records/2000246
Francois de Soete, “From a Kingdom of Gold to One of Iron and Rust: Political Violence and the Fall of the Roman Empire,” Kwansei Gakuin University Humanities Review 26 (2021): 87-106. URL to be provided by the instructor.
Students can access the following textbooks from the SIS library (it is NOT necessary for students to purchase the following textbooks):
Joshua Cole and Carol Symes, Western Civilizations, 20e Combined Volume, (New York: W.W Norton & Company, 2020). [Available at the SIS Library] John Baylis, Steve Smith, and Patricia Owens, “The Globalization of World Politics, Eighth Edition” (Oxford University Press, 2020). [Available at the SIS Library]

Francois de Soete, “From a Kingdom of Gold to One of Iron and Rust: Political Violence and the Fall of the Roman Empire,” Kwansei Gakuin University Humanities Review 26 (2021): 87-106. URL to be provided by the instructor.

学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: Each course is positioned within the curriculum in accordance with the diploma policy andcurriculum policy established by each undergraduate/graduate school. By proceeding with the study of this course with an awareness of the DP and CP, the following abilities expected by each undergraduate/graduate school will be developed.Global Studies:1. Understanding and analytical skills regarding international issues: the ability to understand and analyze various regions of the world from different perspectives in the humanities and social sciences.2. Problem-solving skills: the ability to proactively identify problems and solve them based on appropriate methods.3. Intercultural understanding and multicultural coexistence: the ability to be sensitive to different cultures and to coexist with diverse cultures.
